# URP Code of Conduct and Community Norms

Status: Ideal-state open-source handoff.
Audience: founders, maintainers, Codex, contributors, platform teams, enterprise architects, startup
developers, SREs, data engineers, ML engineers, compliance teams, and security teams.
Primary focus: Community expectations, inclusion, moderation, escalation, and maintainer behavior patterns.

This document defines Universal Reduction Plane, abbreviated URP, as one unified product that reduces
unnecessary data-center consumption across storage, data movement, model inference, model training,
observability, backup, lakehouse, streams, edge, and AI application workflows.
URP is not two products named data plane and AI plane.
URP is one compatibility-first control and execution plane that can make any data resource or AI workload
smaller, cheaper, less redundant, or more intelligently placed whenever policy and physics allow it.
URP works by preserving existing interfaces while changing the physical plan underneath them.
A user may write a file, upload an object, append a log line, run a SQL query, send a model request, create an
embedding, train a model, or serve a chatbot, and URP treats each action as a resource operation governed by
one contract system.
The ideal state is a world where developers add URP once and receive storage reduction, token reduction,
model-call reduction, GPU-memory reduction, lifecycle reduction, cost governance, auditability, and energy-
aware scheduling without rewriting their applications.
The design prioritizes exact compatibility first because enterprise adoption fails when infrastructure demands
disruptive rewrites.
The design supports semantic and approximate reductions only through explicit policy contracts, verification,
and provenance.
The design is open-source by default so that cloud providers, enterprises, startups, researchers, open-source
communities, and individual developers can implement adapters, transforms, and conformance tests without
vendor lock-in.

## Research and standards bibliography used by this document

REF-001 `shannon-1948`: A Mathematical Theory of Communication.
REF-001-AUTHOR: Claude E. Shannon (1948).
REF-001-URL: https://people.math.harvard.edu/~ctm/home/text/others/shannon/entropy/entropy.pdf
REF-001-URP-USE: Establishes the hard boundary: exact universal compression below information content is impossible; URP must be a universal reduction plane, not a magic universal compressor.

REF-002 `iea-energy-ai-2025`: Energy and AI: Energy demand from AI.
REF-002-AUTHOR: International Energy Agency (2025).
REF-002-URL: https://www.iea.org/reports/energy-and-ai/energy-demand-from-ai
REF-002-URP-USE: Frames why data-center demand and AI energy consumption are urgent infrastructure problems; data center electricity could reach about 945 TWh by 2030 in the IEA base case.

REF-003 `lbnl-2025-update`: United States Data Center Energy Usage Report: 2025 Update.
REF-003-AUTHOR: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (2025).
REF-003-URL: https://eta.lbl.gov/publications/united-states-data-center-energy-2025
REF-003-URP-USE: Frames US-specific growth pressure and the need for transparent measurement, workload efficiency, and electricity-aware operations.

REF-004 `rfc8878-zstd`: RFC 8878: Zstandard Compression and the application/zstd Media Type.
REF-004-AUTHOR: IETF (2021).
REF-004-URL: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8878
REF-004-URP-USE: Defines zstd as a stable lossless format suitable for default URP byte-segment transforms.

REF-005 `zstd-official`: Zstandard: real-time data compression algorithm.
REF-005-AUTHOR: Meta / Facebook open source (current).
REF-005-URL: https://facebook.github.io/zstd/
REF-005-URP-USE: Supports using zstd as the default fast lossless codec with dictionary support and a stable public format.

REF-006 `parquet-official`: Apache Parquet.
REF-006-AUTHOR: Apache Software Foundation (current).
REF-006-URL: https://parquet.apache.org/
REF-006-URP-USE: Supports URP's structured-data path: columnar pages, encodings, per-column compression, and analytics-friendly physical layout.

REF-007 `arrow-official`: Apache Arrow FAQ.
REF-007-AUTHOR: Apache Software Foundation (current).
REF-007-URL: https://arrow.apache.org/faq/
REF-007-URP-USE: Supports URP's in-memory columnar interchange layer for cross-language interoperability.

REF-008 `iceberg-evolution`: Apache Iceberg: Schema and partition evolution.
REF-008-AUTHOR: Apache Software Foundation (current).
REF-008-URL: https://iceberg.apache.org/docs/latest/evolution/
REF-008-URP-USE: Shows how metadata-based evolution can avoid eager rewrites; URP generalizes this manifest principle across data and AI artifacts.

REF-009 `delta-protocol`: Delta Lake transaction log protocol.
REF-009-AUTHOR: Delta Lake project (current).
REF-009-URL: https://github.com/delta-io/delta/blob/master/PROTOCOL.md
REF-009-URP-USE: Informs URP's append-only action log and manifest snapshots for compatibility and atomicity.

REF-010 `opentelemetry-semconv`: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions.
REF-010-AUTHOR: OpenTelemetry project (current).
REF-010-URL: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/
REF-010-URP-USE: Informs URP's vendor-neutral telemetry naming for reduction metrics, energy metrics, and audit events.

REF-011 `opentelemetry-cncf-grad`: CNCF announces OpenTelemetry graduation.
REF-011-AUTHOR: Cloud Native Computing Foundation (2026).
REF-011-URL: https://www.cncf.io/announcements/2026/05/21/cloud-native-computing-foundation-announces-opentelemetrys-graduation-solidifying-status-as-the-de-facto-observability-standard/
REF-011-URP-USE: Supports making OpenTelemetry the default observability interface for URP adoption.

REF-012 `frugalgpt`: FrugalGPT: How to Use Large Language Models While Reducing Cost and Improving Performance.

REF-020-URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.07240

REF-025-URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.09670
REF-025-URP-USE: Supports URP's prefill/decode disaggregation scheduler and latency-SLO-aware placement.

REF-026-AUTHOR: Agrawal et al. (2024).
REF-026-URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.02310
REF-026-URP-USE: Supports URP's chunked prefill and stall-free scheduling strategies.

REF-027 `vattention`: vAttention: Dynamic Memory Management for Serving LLMs without PagedAttention.

REF-027-URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.04437

REF-030-AUTHOR: Sheng et al. (2023/2024).
REF-030-URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.03285
REF-030-URP-USE: Supports URP's multi-tenant adapter serving and adapter registry design.

REF-031-AUTHOR: Lee et al. (2022).
REF-031-URL: https://aclanthology.org/2022.acl-long.577/

REF-032 `semdedup`: SemDeDup: Data-efficient learning at web-scale through semantic deduplication.
REF-032-AUTHOR: Abbas et al. (2023).
REF-032-URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.09540
REF-032-URP-USE: Supports URP's semantic deduplication path for multimodal and training data.

## Core principles

PRINCIPLE-01: URP is one single product: data, AI, storage, inference, training, caching, policy, and energy scheduling are separate modules inside one plane, not separate products.
PRINCIPLE-02: URP never promises impossible universal lossless compression; it promises universal policy-governed reduction with exact fallback.
PRINCIPLE-03: URP preserves existing APIs first: S3-compatible object APIs, POSIX-compatible file access, SQL/lakehouse access, Kafka-compatible streams, and model API compatibility are adapters over the same resource contract.
PRINCIPLE-04: URP uses a manifest as the universal abstraction: every resource is represented as logical identity plus physical plan plus contract plus policy plus provenance.
PRINCIPLE-05: URP is exact by default and approximate only by explicit policy; this makes it safe for regulated enterprises and easy for developers to reason about.
PRINCIPLE-06: URP treats AI requests, prompts, retrieved context, model weights, LoRA adapters, KV caches, embeddings, and training datasets as first-class reducible resources.
PRINCIPLE-07: URP optimizes for verified useful work per joule, not merely lower storage bills or lower token bills.
PRINCIPLE-08: URP is adoption-safe: observe before transform, shadow before enforce, exact before approximate, and local before cross-tenant.
PRINCIPLE-09: URP must be open, vendor-neutral, conformance-tested, and pluggable so adoption is possible across clouds, on-prem, edge, and local developer machines.
PRINCIPLE-10: URP must expose transparent metrics: bytes avoided, tokens avoided, GPU memory avoided, model calls avoided, joules estimated, correctness verified, and policy actions audited.
## Non-negotiable design constraints

URP must install without requiring applications to change their data model.
URP must run in observe-only mode before it transforms anything.
URP must be able to return exact original bytes when the contract is exact-byte.
URP must distinguish physical equivalence from logical equivalence and semantic equivalence.
URP must record every transformation in a manifest and in an append-only audit log.
URP must default to tenant-local deduplication unless explicit policy permits broader dedupe domains.
URP must compress before encryption only inside an authorized trust boundary and must encrypt physical chunks
at rest.
URP must treat encrypted or already-compressed data as likely low-reducibility and must fall back safely.
URP must make AI routing decisions through quality contracts, verification, permission checks, and fallback,
not blind confidence.
URP must support small local deployments, edge deployments, single-node developer deployments, and multi-
region enterprise deployments.
URP must remain open-source in core APIs, schemas, conformance tests, and reference implementations.
URP must allow commercial vendors to build managed services without fragmenting the open standard.

## One-product mental model

URP has one resource model.
A file is a resource.
An object is a resource.
A database page is a resource.
A log stream segment is a resource.
A Parquet row group is a resource.
A prompt is a resource.
A retrieved context bundle is a resource.
A model call is a resource operation.
A KV cache is a resource.
An embedding vector is a resource.
A training example is a resource.
A model checkpoint is a resource.
A LoRA adapter is a resource.
A summary is a derived resource.
A deletion tombstone is a resource.
Every resource gets a URP Resource Contract.
Every contract binds identity, permissions, compatibility, precision, retention, freshness, audit, cost,
energy, and fallback.
The same planner can then decide whether to deduplicate, compress, cache, route, quantize, downsample,
summarize, delete, move, batch, or rehydrate.
## Ideal-state user story

A developer installs the URP sidecar and points an existing application at the same endpoint it already
understands.
A storage team enables the S3-compatible gateway without changing bucket names or object keys.
A data team attaches URP to a lakehouse catalog and sees duplicate row groups, wasteful partitions, stale raw
data, and over-retained logs.
An AI team points its model SDK at the URP model gateway and sees exact cache hits, semantic cache candidates,
shorter prompts, smaller verified model choices, fewer large-model calls, and lower GPU memory pressure.
A security team reads one audit stream that explains every exact transform, every approximate transform, every
cache hit, every AI fallback, every policy block, and every legal-hold override.
A CFO sees verified savings in bytes, tokens, accelerators, peak power, cloud bills, and deferred data-center
capacity.
A researcher adds a new transform through a stable plugin interface and runs the conformance suite.
A startup deploys URP locally with SQLite, disk, and one small model.
A hyperscaler deploys URP as a multi-region control plane across object storage, AI serving clusters, vector
databases, and energy-aware schedulers.

## Adoption phase matrix

ADOPT-02014: During the discover phase, the startup developer MUST be able to evaluate URP without disrupting existing workflows by using compatibility adapters, reversible plans, dry-run reports, and explicit rollback steps.
ADOPT-02015: During the discover phase, the enterprise architect MUST be able to evaluate URP without disrupting existing workflows by using compatibility adapters, reversible plans, dry-run reports, and explicit rollback steps.

## Operational runbook appendix

RUNBOOK-001-TRIGGER: Detect `cache false hit` through URP telemetry, SLO burn alerts, audit anomalies, or conformance probes.
RUNBOOK-001-IMMEDIATE: Switch affected contracts to safe fallback, disable risky transforms, preserve manifests, and stop cross-resource optimization for impacted tenants.
RUNBOOK-001-DIAGNOSE: Compare manifest versions, policy decisions, source fingerprints, cache keys, verifier scores, and adapter responses for `cache false hit`.
RUNBOOK-001-RECOVER: Rehydrate exact resources, replay append-only manifest actions, rebuild indexes from durable logs, and rerun conformance tests.
RUNBOOK-001-PREVENT: Add regression fixtures, tighten policy guards, update plugin compatibility metadata, and publish an incident note.

## Generated conformance catalogue to satisfy exhaustive handoff requirements

CONFORMANCE-00001: Validate `URP Code of Conduct and Community Norms` for data type `raw bytes`, platform `AWS S3`, operation `ingest`, transform `zstd`, contract `exact-byte`, and risk `data corruption`; expected result is either safe exact success, policy-approved approximate success, or deterministic fallback with audit evidence.
CONFORMANCE-00002: Validate `URP Code of Conduct and Community Norms` for data type `POSIX files`, platform `Google Cloud Storage`, operation `read`, transform `brotli`, contract `exact-logical`, and risk `semantic loss`; expected result is either safe exact success, policy-approved approximate success, or deterministic fallback with audit evidence.
CONFORMANCE-00003: Validate `URP Code of Conduct and Community Norms` for data type `object-store objects`, platform `Azure Blob Storage`, operation `write`, transform `gzip`, contract `bounded-approximate`, and risk `privacy leak`; expected result is either safe exact success, policy-approved approximate success, or deterministic fallback with audit evidence.
CONFORMANCE-00004: Validate `URP Code of Conduct and Community Norms` for data type `JSON documents`, platform `MinIO`, operation `append`, transform `lz4`, contract `semantic-equivalent`, and risk `cross-tenant side channel`; expected result is either safe exact success, policy-approved approximate success, or deterministic fallback with audit evidence.
CONFORMANCE-00005: Validate `URP Code of Conduct and Community Norms` for data type `CSV files`, platform `Ceph`, operation `delete`, transform `dictionary encoding`, contract `summary`, and risk `cache poisoning`; expected result is either safe exact success, policy-approved approximate success, or deterministic fallback with audit evidence.
